result:
Done. I built and committed a persistent JSON REST API for a `notes` resource.

## What was built

**Zero external dependencies** — uses Node's built-in `node:http` server and `node:sqlite` (on-disk file at `data/notes.db`, overridable via `NOTES_DB_PATH`).

- `src/db.js` — opens/creates the SQLite database and exposes a small store (create/list/get/update/delete) over prepared statements.
- `src/app.js` — the HTTP request handler: routing, JSON body parsing (with size cap), and validation.
- `src/server.js` — wires the store to the server, ensures the data dir exists, listens on `PORT` (default 3000), graceful shutdown.
- `test/notes.test.js` — `node:test` suite.

## Behavior (per spec)

| Endpoint | Result |
|---|---|
| `POST /notes` | validates `title`/`body` are non-empty strings → `201` with `{id, title, body, createdAt}` (UUID id, ISO timestamp); invalid → `400` |
| `GET /notes` | `200` array of all notes |
| `GET /notes/:id` | `200` note, or `404` |
| `PUT /notes/:id` | accepts `title` and/or `body`, validates non-empty → `200` updated; invalid → `400`; absent → `404` |
| `DELETE /notes/:id` | `204`, or `404` |

Added the npm `start` script (and a `test` script).

## Verification

- All 7 tests pass (`npm test`), including an explicit restart-persistence test.
- Live `curl` smoke test confirmed create → restart server → `GET` returns the same note → delete → `404`, plus the `400` validation path.

Committed as `8f0588b` on `main`. I left the pre-existing `.agents/` deletions from the checkout untouched, staging only my new/modified files.
